name: Alzheimer’s Disease
summary: Alzheimer’s disease is a neurodegenerative condition with late-onset polygenic architecture and notable loci such as APOE. GENARCH provides population-level educational summaries only.
adolescent_relevance: Typically manifests later in life; included to illustrate neuroinflammation and genetic architecture concepts in an educational framing.

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS discussed as population-level genetic liability; GENARCH does not provide individual predictions.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Sleep patterns are associated with cognitive outcomes in population studies; causal direction is complex.
Air pollution is studied in relation to neurocognitive outcomes; evidence varies by design.
Vascular and cardiometabolic factors influence brain health in cohorts.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: Many AD genetics studies are European-ancestry dominated historically.
population_equity.transferability_notes: Effect estimates can vary across populations due to LD and ascertainment.
population_equity.data_gaps: Diagnosis and exposure measurement differ across settings.

references.title: Genome-wide meta-analysis of Alzheimer’s disease identifies risk loci and highlights immune pathways
Sleep duration and health outcomes: overview of population evidence (review)
Air pollution and health: evidence and mechanisms (review)
references.journal: Journal of the Air & Waste Management Association

references.title: WHO guidelines on physical activity and sedentary behaviour
